dialogue.text: Our land remembers the hands that farm it, the paths traders walk, and the families who care for one another. Before strangers arrive, listen closely to what already lives here.
A good harvest depends on patience, rain, soil, and many hands. What a family grows is shared through work, exchange, and care.

npcs.journal_entry: Village Life: Farming, grazing, and trade supported communities long before colonial rule. Practices differed across Kenya's regions and environments.

dialogue.text: News travels with people as surely as goods do. Paths connect villages, markets, and distant communities; trade carries skills, stories, and relationships.

npcs.journal_entry: Trade and Exchange: Communities across the region maintained networks of exchange, communication, and travel.

dialogue.text: Every pattern takes time. I learn by watching older hands, then by making mistakes of my own. Skills are carried forward when people teach one another.

npcs.journal_entry: Knowledge and Craft: Practical knowledge is passed between generations through observation, practice, and community teaching.
